prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT large object standing FREE on a small flat patch of pale-blue crystalline ice — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a glowing crystal obelisk 2) an ice-encased ruined tower 3) a towering aquamarine crystal cluster 4) a giant glowing crystal spire 5) a great geode split with glowing crystals 6) a tall spiral ice horn 7) a tall cluster of ice blades 8) a frozen waterfall column 9) a leaning berg of blue glacier ice 10) an angular crystal monolith. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
